methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
SiPearl, a Series A chips and semiconductors company founded by Philippe Notton, has raised €130M to date. Valuation analysis relies on a comparable universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage private peers (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) alongside 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

The peer-median method is applicable and yields a $1.5B implied valuation. The four comparables span a range of $500M to $5.0B, with Etched at the high end ($5.0B), d-Matrix at $2.0B, Substrate at $1.0B, and Rain AI at the low end ($500M). This dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology focus and funding trajectory within the frontier chip segment. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 25.3x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied given the absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to one applicable method, supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on $1.5B. Key uncertainties include the degree to which SiPearl's technical differentiation and addressable market align with outlier peers like Etched versus the median cohort, and whether private-market pricing in this segment has begun to normalize following 2023–2024 funding intensity. Revenue trajectory and go-to-market validation remain primary value drivers absent from the current dataset.
